# PyAutoFit Assistant

> The PyAutoFit Assistant is an AI assistant for statistical inference in your own scientific domain — agent instructions, task skills, and a reference wiki in one cloned repo you drive by conversation. This file is the front door: it routes you by intent — start a chat session, see what the assistant can do, read the reference, or jump to runnable examples.

## Using it in chat (start here)

Point a coding agent or browser chat (ChatGPT, Claude) at this repo and open with:

```text
I'd like to use the PyAutoFit Assistant for this conversation:
https://github.com/PyAutoLabs/autofit_assistant

Please familiarize yourself with the project and use its guidance and statistical
reference material when answering my questions. I'll provide any local files or results you need.
```

Read order: [AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md) → [skills/README.md](./skills/README.md) → the relevant skill → [wiki/](./wiki). If the chat can't browse GitHub, paste this file and `AGENTS.md`. The assistant can route to examples, explain Bayesian inference, review pasted scripts/errors/figures, and draft code; it cannot run code, inspect your files, or guarantee drafts against your installed PyAutoFit version. For repeated edits, live execution, or multi-session project state, switch to a local coding agent.

## Agent instructions (canonical)

- [AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md): safety rules, modes, domain adaptation, and repository conventions — the source of truth.

## What it can do

- [skills/README.md](./skills/README.md): one-line index of every task skill. The
  defining ones: wrap your likelihood code, ingest your field's papers, adapt to your
  domain.

## Reference

- [wiki/core/index.md](./wiki/core/index.md): curated PyAutoFit + statistics reference — stack, inference concepts (priors, samplers, evidence, EP), operations.
- [wiki/literature/index.md](./wiki/literature/index.md): *your* domain's science wiki — ships near-empty by design and grows as you ingest your field's papers.

## Runnable examples & tutorials (elsewhere)

- [autofit_workspace](https://github.com/PyAutoLabs/autofit_workspace): end-to-end example scripts, cookbooks and notebooks.
- [HowToFit](https://github.com/PyAutoLabs/HowToFit): from-first-principles model-fitting lecture series.
